title: Weigh the cups

placement.where: replaces NCERT Grade 6 Science Ch.6 Activity 6.8 'How heavy? How light?' — same volume of different materials has different mass/weight; the student weighs each choice on a balance and picks the heavier one

windowMethods: weigh(idOrArray) — THE do-verb: pick the heavier choice for the CURRENT question; id is a choice id/label (e.g. 'A'|'B'|'C','water','iron') or an array; correct locks it, an array auto-advances.
compare(a,b | array) — narrate which of the given current-question choices is heavier WITHOUT locking the answer (a preview/hint); a,b are choice ids/labels, or pass one array.
next() — advance to the next question after a correct weigh(); on the last question it finishes the quiz.

studentControls.control: Pick a choice for the current question (Cup A/B/C, Cotton/Water, Iron/Feathers/Same)
studentControls.effect: lights the chosen item, tips the balance and shows its live weight read-out; a correct pick locks green with a one-line reason and reveals the Next button, a wrong pick shakes red with a hint and lets the student retry

studentControls.control: Next Question / See Results button (after a correct pick)
studentControls.effect: advances to the next weighing question or to the summary card
clears score, solved questions and selection and returns to the first question

questions.prompt: Three identical half-filled cups (water / sand / pebbles) — which is heaviest?
questions.reason: Pebbles are denser than sand or water, so the same volume of pebbles has the most mass.

questions.prompt: Half a cup of cotton wool vs half a cup of water — which has more mass?
questions.reason: Water is much denser than cotton wool. The same volume of water has far more mass.

questions.prompt: 1 kg of iron vs 1 kg of feathers — which has more mass?
questions.reason: 1 kg is 1 kg! Mass is the amount of matter. The material and size do not change the stated mass.

answerKey.argResolution: weigh()/select()/compare()/highlight() resolve an arg against the CURRENT question's choice ids and labels first, then a global alias map (e.g. 'pebbles'->C, 'heaviest'->the current correct answer, and qualified 'qid:choice' strings). Each 'do' verb accepts a single id OR an array so the agent can credit several picks at once.
answerKey.rule: Same volume of a denser material has more mass/weight. A balance tips toward the heavier pan; equal stated masses keep it level.
answerKey.operatorModeNote: ai = focused DEMO: the student choice buttons are hidden and inert (the agent drives via weigh()/compare()/highlight()) BUT the weighing phenomenon stays — the balance/cups scene and the live weight read-out remain visible in both modes; a '👩‍🏫 watch' caption + chip show. student = full interactive controls. Default student.

additionalProps.finalMessage: Mass = the amount of matter in an object. More matter packed in the same space means more mass. A kilogram is a kilogram — no matter what material it is!

scientificFacts: The same amount of space (volume) filled with a denser material holds more matter, so it weighs more.
Pebbles are denser than sand, and sand is denser than water — equal cups of them have different masses.
Mass is the amount of matter in an object; 1 kilogram of iron and 1 kilogram of feathers have exactly the same mass.
A balance tips down on the heavier side and stays level when both sides have equal mass.
